purpose: Plan a PDF-to-PPT deck using img-gen + Presentation by confirming PDF scope, PPT production idea (PDF-content idea or user-provided custom idea), exactly one visual source (style description, logo-derived template, or template-library PPT reuse), and speaker-notes preference; allocating slide counts by content volume and importance; generating an outline; applying the resolved production idea and reusable template system; saving generated style-description or logo-derived templates as .pptx files for reuse/export; letting Presentation define image requirements; using img-gen for backgrounds and Presentation-requested image assets; and batching decks over 10 slides.

implementation_technology.img_gen_role: Generate slide background images, style-description or template decorative backgrounds or motifs, and any additional image assets requested by Presentation, following Presentation-provided dimensions, resolved visual source, visual references, and content safe areas.
implementation_technology.presentation_role: Handle all remaining work: plan execution, visual-source resolution, template-library PPT reuse, style-description template creation, logo/prompt-based template creation, saving generated templates as reusable .pptx files, PPT creation, layout, inserting generated images, text and PowerPoint objects, optional speaker notes, page numbering, batch assembly, and merging.
implementation_technology.critical_rule: Resolve the PPT production idea and exactly one visual-source/template strategy before generation. img-gen produces backgrounds, template decorative image assets, and Presentation-requested image assets; Presentation handles deck structure, layout, content placement, and final assembly.

pdf_scope.question: Which part of the PDF should be used to generate the PPT?
pdf_scope.notes: Do not proceed to slide allocation, outline generation, template creation/selection, img-gen, or PPT creation until this is known.

ppt_production_idea.question: Should the PPT production idea follow the PDF content, or should it follow a user-provided custom idea?

pdf_content_idea.description: Infer the storyline, emphasis, slide order, and structure from the confirmed PDF scope, content density, importance, and narrative priority.
pdf_content_idea.notes: Use this when the user wants the PPT logic to be organized from the PDF content.

custom_user_idea.notes: Collect the custom production idea before slide allocation and outline generation.

ppt_production_idea.notes: Do not generate PPT files until this production idea is resolved.

visual_source_strategy.question: Which single visual source should be used: style description, logo-derived template, or template-library PPT reuse?

style_description.notes: Use this path only when the user wants a style description as the visual source. Generate a reusable template system with Presentation and save it as a .pptx before or alongside the final deck.

logo_derived_template.notes: Require both a logo asset/reference and a template prompt. The logo/prompt is the style source; do not ask for a separate style description unless the user explicitly requests an override. Save the generated template as a reusable .pptx.

reuse_template_library.notes: Ask the user to choose or describe the preferred library PPT template before generation. The selected template is the style source; do not ask for a separate style description.

visual_source_strategy.notes: Do not generate PPT files until exactly one visual-source strategy is resolved. Style description, logo-derived styling, and template-library reuse are alternatives, not cumulative requirements.

speaker_notes.question: Should speaker notes be generated for the PPT?
speaker_notes.usage: If true, create concise presenter notes for each slide. If false, do not add speaker notes.

style_notes.background_art_direction: Use the resolved visual source and resolved PPT production idea; default to academic minimalist style only when the selected visual source is style_description and no style prompt is provided. Presentation must define image dimensions, safe areas, and visual requirements before img-gen is called.
style_notes.content_overlay_rules: Presentation controls readable safe areas, high contrast, and consistent spacing for slide content.

deck_plan.speaker_notes_rule: Generate notes only if preflight_questions.speaker_notes.value is true.

ppt_production_idea.application_rule: Resolve the PPT production idea before slide allocation and outline generation so page allocation, section order, content emphasis, and visual hierarchy follow the chosen idea.

resolved_visual_source.application_rule: Resolve exactly one visual source before per-slide background generation. For style-description or logo-derived paths, save the generated reusable template system as a .pptx and record the path. For template-library reuse, use the selected .pptx as the style source without asking for a separate style description.

negative_prompt_or_constraints: Keep background complexity low enough for clear slide content.
Leave clean, high-contrast areas for PowerPoint content.
img_gen_background.resolved_visual_source_style: style_description: academic minimalist style (default only when style-description path is selected)

template_application.template_layout_type: cover | agenda | section divider | content | visual/chart | summary | closing/Q&A

batch_plan.batch_size_rule: 5 slides for design-heavy decks; 6-8 slides for normal academic/report decks; up to 10 slides only when layouts are simple.

batches.visual_style_notes: Use the resolved visual-source/template strategy and resolved visual source notes consistently across all Presentation layouts, img-gen backgrounds, and Presentation-requested image assets.
batches.speaker_notes_behavior: Generate only if requested in preflight_questions.speaker_notes.value.
batch_checks: production idea matches the resolved PDF-content or custom-user idea
titles and main text are present
img-gen backgrounds are present or intentionally selected
extra img-gen image assets were requested by Presentation
background safe areas support readable content
speaker notes presence matches user preference
template source or generated template system matches other batches
resolved visual source matches other batches
batches.production_idea_notes: Use the resolved PPT production idea consistently for slide order, emphasis, section transitions, and visual hierarchy in this batch.

merge_checks: PPT production idea is consistent across all slides and batches
visual-source/template strategy is consistent across all slides and batches
background and Presentation-requested image asset art direction is consistent
speaker notes presence or absence matches user preference

quality_checks: PDF scope was confirmed before generation
PPT production idea was confirmed before generation
Visual-source/template strategy was confirmed before generation
Generated templates from style-description or logo-derived paths were saved as reusable .pptx files and their paths were recorded
No separate style-description prompt was required when logo-derived template or template-library PPT reuse was selected
Exactly one visual source was selected: style description, logo-derived template, or template-library PPT reuse
A single visual source was confirmed: style description, logo-derived template, or template-library PPT reuse
implementation technology is img-gen + Presentation
Selected template-library PPT or generated style-description/logo-derived template was applied through Presentation before slide generation
Generated templates from style description or logo were saved as reusable .pptx files; logo-based template generation used a logo asset/reference and prompt when requested
speaker-notes preference was confirmed or defaulted to no speaker notes
Slide allocation and outline follow the resolved PDF-content or custom-user production idea
slide count matches the planned or requested count
slide allocation reflects PDF content volume and importance
slide order matches the outline
every slide has an img-gen background or intentionally selected background
any additional img-gen image assets were requested by Presentation
main PDF-derived or outline-derived content is represented
critical title/key point/conclusion is represented clearly
speaker notes are present only if requested
page numbers are continuous
resolved visual-source styling is consistent across batches
backgrounds leave enough contrast and whitespace for content
recovery_strategy: Reallocate slides if a section is too compressed or too sparse.
Regenerate weak slide backgrounds with larger safe areas or lower visual complexity.
Reduce text density and convert long paragraphs into concise bullets.
Use PDF visuals as references or supporting images instead of full-slide screenshots.
Regenerate only the weak slide or batch rather than rebuilding the full deck.
